entry.title: Gamechanger Alert: Meet SAP Joule Studio for Real-World Impact
entry.content:
As someone who's still fairly new to SAP and learning about all it has to offer, I recently came across SAP Joule Studio—a tool that honestly surprised me. It’s part of SAP’s growing focus on AI, but instead of being just a tech buzzword, it’s something practical. Something helpful. Something that actually makes life easier.

So I thought I’d share what I’ve learned so far, for anyone else who’s curious like me.

 

What is SAP Joule Studio?

SAP Joule Studio is a low-code/no-code platform that lets you create AI-powered agents—little digital helpers that live inside your SAP system and support everyday tasks. These agents can:

  • Answer questions using real SAP data
  • Send reminders or alerts
  • Trigger workflows based on events
  • Help users navigate complex processes

The best part? You don’t need to be a developer to build one.

All you need is a good understanding of a business problem, and Joule Studio helps you turn that into a solution using easy visual tools.

 

Why This Is a Game-Changer (With Real Use Cases)

What really got me excited was seeing how real-world teams can use this tool to solve day-to-day challenges. Here are a few examples that really stood out to me:

✅HR & Employee Self-Service

  • Use Case: An AI agent that answers common HR questions like “How many vacation days do I have left?”, “What’s the process to update my bank details?”, or “When does my probation end?”
  • Impact: Reduces emails to HR, gives instant answers, and improves employee satisfaction.

✅Onboarding Assistant

  • Use Case: An AI agent that guides a new joiner through their first week—showing them how to complete paperwork, introducing them to teammates, and sending daily checklists.
  • Impact: Makes onboarding smoother, especially for remote hires or global teams.

✅Procurement Alerts

  • Use Case: A smart assistant that watches for delays from key suppliers and notifies procurement managers if a PO is stuck or overdue.
  • Impact: Keeps procurement responsive and improves relationships with vendors.

✅Project Management Summary

  • Use Case: A daily AI briefing that tells project leads about overdue tasks, timesheet gaps, or resource bottlenecks.
  • Impact: Saves hours of manual tracking and keeps teams aligned.

✅Compliance Checker

  • Use Case: A bot that checks which employees haven't completed their mandatory training or signed policy documents, and sends them a reminder.
  • Impact: Helps HR stay compliant without manual follow-ups.

✅IT Helpdesk Agent

  • Use Case: An agent that answers questions like “How do I reset my SAP password?” or “Where do I raise a ticket?”
  • Impact: Cuts down ticket volume for IT support and empowers users.

These aren’t just theoretical—they’re real issues we see in companies every day. And Joule Studio gives you a way to solve them quickly, without coding or waiting on IT.

 

Why Clients Will Love It Too

For clients, Joule Studio brings:

  • Self-service power to their SAP users
  • Less dependency on IT for small tasks
  • Smarter workflows with reminders, alerts, and guidance
  • Faster onboarding and training for new team members
  • Consistency across geographies and business units

AI becomes something helpful, not something scary or complex. It empowers users instead of overwhelming them.

 

Where You Can See It in Action

If you're like me and prefer learning visually, I highly recommend this official demo:

 SAP Joule Studio – AI for Everyone (YouTube)

You can also explore SAP’s official product page for more examples and how-to guides:
 SAP Joule Studio – Official Page

These are great starting points with visuals and real examples.

 

Getting Started with Joule Studio

If you’re ready to try it:

  1. Make sure your system is running on SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform).
  2. Go into SAP Build and access Joule Studio.
  3. Start by creating a small AI agent for a common task.
  4. Train it using real process examples and test in Preview mode.
  5. Roll it out with the right user permissions.

 

 

 

Remember—you don’t need to be technical. You just need to understand your business and what people are struggling with. Joule Studio helps you close that gap.

 

Final Thoughts (From One Learner to Another)

I’m still exploring SAP, still learning, and still asking questions. But tools like Joule Studio give me hope, not just for where tech is going, but for how we can make it work for people.

This isn’t about replacing humans—it’s about supporting them with small, smart helpers that remove friction and free up time.

If you're also exploring SAP tools and wondering where to start with AI, this might just be the right place. Try building a simple use case and see what it unlocks for your team.

And if you’ve tried it, I’d really love to hear what you’ve built. Let’s share and learn together, because the best ideas often come from the people closest to the problem.

 

#SAPJoule, #JouleStudio, #SAPBTP, #AIinSAP, #Gamechanger, #NoCodeAI, #SAPBuild, #EnterpriseAI, #DigitalTransformation, #SAPCommunity

entry.title: Joule based SAP HANA Application Migration Assistant - Transforming with Gen AI
entry.content:
In Early 2024, released first version of the SAP Business Application Studio extension which  Automates conversion of SAP HANA Database artifacts to SAP HANA Cloud artifacts.   SAP HANA  database objects (such as .hdbcds, .hdbtable, .hdbview, etc.) are transformed into their corresponding SAP CAP compliant database artifacts (e.g., .cds models).

The Q2-2025  release , enables service layer migration using GenAI capabilities in SAP Build Code. The assistant analyses SAP XS objects - xsodata ,xsjslib, xsjs and  files and generate corresponding service.cds, service.js and custom handler implementations under the CAP service layer. These AI capabilities  accelerates the migration of service layer while preserving structure and functional behaviour where possible. A sample conversion of xsodata to SAP CAP service definition is shown below. 

poWorklist.xsodata

service namespace "com.sap.openSAP.hana5.example.service"  { 
  "sap.hana.democontent.epm.models::PO_HEADER_EN" as "PO_WORKLIST" 
   keys ("PURCHASEORDERID","PURCHASEORDERITEM");
   
   "sap.hana.democontent.epm.models::PO_ITEM" as "PurchaseOrderItem" keys ("PURCHASEORDERID","PURCHASEORDERITEM");
   
   "PO.HeaderView" as "PurchaseOrderHeader" keys("PurchaseOrderId")
   navigates (     
        "PurchaseOrderHeader_PurchaseOrderItem" as "PurchaseOrderItems" from principal);

   association "PurchaseOrderHeader_PurchaseOrderItem"
      principal "PurchaseOrderHeader"("PurchaseOrderId") multiplicity "1"
      dependent "PurchaseOrderItem"("PurchaseOrderId") multiplicity "*";

}

service.cds

//converted from: /users/projects/hana-shine-xsa/core-xsjs/lib/xsodata/poWorklist.xsodata
service poWorklist @(path:'/poWorklist') {
    entity PO_WORKLIST as projection on SAP_HANA_DEMOCONTENT_EPM_MODELS_PO_HEADER_EN {
        *,
        PurchaseOrderItems: association to many PurchaseOrderItem on PurchaseOrderItems.PurchaseOrderId = $self.PURCHASEORDERID
    };

    entity PurchaseOrderItem as projection on SAP_HANA_DEMOCONTENT_EPM_MODELS_PO_ITEM;

    entity PurchaseOrderHeader as projection on PO.HeaderView {
        *,
        PurchaseOrderItems: association to many PurchaseOrderItem on PurchaseOrderItems.PurchaseOrderId = $self.PurchaseOrderId
    };
}

The converted code includes comments linking back to the original source files - making it easy for users to verify that key business logic has been accurately transformed.  In the similar way, all xsjslib and xsjs files are re-written as SAP CAP custom handlers. The resulting  modularised SAP CAP compliant project mirrors the original directory structure, ensuring familiarity and easy traceability.  All SAP HANA objects  will be adjusted automatically to follow SAP HANA Cloud naming conventions.

Additionally, the assistant generates a detailed report outlining recommended post-migration steps to guide users through final adjustments

As generative AI conversion is not guaranteed to be 100% accurate, human intervention is required post-migration to validate, refine, and productionize the application logic and service definitions.  

References

  • For prerequisites and step by step guide refer to corresponding xsc-to-cap  and xsa-to-cap repositories. 
  • Help Document: Migrating XS Classic Applications to SAP CAP
  • Help Document: SAP HANA XS Advanced Migration Guide

entry.title: New SAP Build Process Automation content for side-by-side extensions with flexible workflows
entry.content:
My colleagues published a SAP Build Process Automation action package "Flexible Workflow Scenario Notifications Action" on SAP Business Accelerator Hub and in the SAP Build Store so that it becomes easier for Citizen Developer to discover and consume it.

It oﬀers an action for an outcome driven extension workﬂow and makes it easier to combine ﬂexible workﬂows from SAP S/4HANA with SAP Build Process Automation capabilities.

From a use case perspective, you could think of including process participants from outside (like external project managers) into the SAP S/4HANA Process. Or you include centrally managed services from SAP BTP like a Rules Service or Joule Skills into your SAP S/4HANA workﬂow project. Or you like to leverage ML services like the recommendation capability of SAP Task Center, which is only available for SAP Build Process Automation tasks? For the blog I took the use case with process participants from outside to explain the possibilities.

In line with side-by-side extensibility ﬂexible workﬂow oﬀers an activitytype "extension step" which can be implemented by flexible workflow scenarios to offer side-by-side extension. It is guideline for all scenario developers to enable it.

I will give a short overview and add some points in addition to the oﬃcial documentation.

The solution architecture gives an overview on the connection between Sales Order Creation in SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP. Over the ﬂexible workﬂow capability of SAP Business Workﬂow it is possible to start an extension process and get back the result / outcome from SAP Build Process Automation .

Configuration in SAP S/4HANA starts with definition of decision reasons for sales orders. In our use case we want to start a flexible workﬂow which is the internal approval solution. After setting an internal approval reason a BAdI checks after saving the Sales Order if an approval is necessary. If true a start event is thrown and checks if a workﬂow is deﬁned in the Manage Workﬂow Application for our scenario. If the scenario contains an extension step, the business process expert is able to configures an extension step with a process deﬁnition from SAP Build Process Automation from the connected BTP subaccount. During runtime when the flow comes to the extension, a SAP Build Process Automation workflow is started via api and a standardize payload.

In the extension workﬂow of our use case we derive the complete sales order header data over an API call to SAP S/4HANA and use this data for Agent Determination. After agent determination via decision table a user decision is modelled with two outcomes.

After the user decision took place we need to set the outcome via script and use it in the action content package. Go to SAP Build Store. Search for "flexible".

Click on the action project for review the details.

Add the action to your project.

To use the action and point to the correct ABAP-Backend-System:

  1. define a destination variable in the SAP Build Process Automation project.
  2. define a destination in the BTP Cockpit to the ABAP-Backend-System(s)
  3. map the destination to the destination variable during deployment of the project.

Define the workflowInstanceId in the context and fill it from the start payload. For the names of the outcomes you need to look up the flexible workflow scenario or check the documentation of the used scenario. If it is custom build in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition or SAP S/4HANA (like in this blog) check your implementation of the extension step and fill the outcomes accordingly.

Please be aware that the development livecycle of the projects on SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP is independent. We work on further enhancements to simplify the integration via the extensibility wizard and also on more features to enrich the possibilities. So stay tuned. A big thanks to all colleagues supporting the integration.

entry.title: A Comprehensive Guide to Transition from SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition to advanced edition
entry.content:
As organizations evolve, the need for more integrated digital workplace solutions becomes paramount. SAP Build Work Zone offers two distinct editions to cater to varying organizational needs: the standard edition and the advanced edition. While the standard edition provides a centralized access point to multiple SAP systems, the advanced edition extends these capabilities by empowering business users. This guide aims to facilitate a seamless transition from the standard to the advanced edition, ensuring that your organization can fully harness the enhanced features of the advanced edition.

Understanding the Differences

Before embarking on the transition, it's essential to comprehend the key distinctions between the two editions to make the most of the expanded set of capabilities in advanced:

  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition: Formerly known as SAP Launchpad service, this edition provides a central access point to multiple SAP cloud and on-premise applications, processes & tasks via different ‘application site’. It also offers different customization and extension capabilities. 

SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition extends the capabilities of the standard edition by empowering business users to create, manage, and publish their own content. It also offers a broader set of widgets for more flexible workpage building. Users can also create interactive communities, known as workspaces, with document access, discussion forums, knowledge base, task collaboration & more.

The following visual offers a more detailed comparison, expanded feature set of the SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition. 

Subscription and Service Plan differences

  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition is often bundled with certain SAP cloud solutions (such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud), providing a streamlined setup for foundational portal and page-building use cases. It supports both subscription-based and consumption-based licensing models, making it suitable for customers seeking fast onboarding with minimal complexity. You can find more details here.
  • On the other hand, SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition is a standalone, premium offering that unlocks enterprise-grade capabilities. It requires a separate license and subscription that follows a follows a consumption-based commercial model. Read more here.

Transition Steps

Transitioning from the standard to the advanced edition involves several critical steps: 

1. Assess current implementation

Begin by thoroughly evaluating your existing SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition setup. Document all active sites, pages, applications, and integrations. Identify any custom developments, branding elements, or specific configurations that are crucial for replication or adaptation in the advanced edition.

2. Plan the transition

Establish clear objectives for the transition, such as enhancing user engagement or improving content management. Engage key stakeholders, including IT teams, business users, and content creators, to gather requirements and address potential concerns.

3. Exporting content from standard edition

It's crucial to export your existing content from standard edition to facilitate a smooth transition to the advanced edition. In the Site Directory, select the desired site, click on 'More Options', and choose 'Export Site' to download the site and its content as a ZIP file. This export includes the site's structure, pages, and associated applications, ensuring that essential components are preserved for import into the advanced edition.

4. Set up the advanced edition environment

It is possible to subscribe to both SAP Build Work Zone, standard and advanced editions within the same subaccount. This makes it easier to set up the advanced edition without disrupting your current standard edition setup.

To get started, subscribe to SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition through the SAP BTP Cockpit. Configure user authentication and provisioning using SAP Cloud Identity Services (Identity Authentication and Identity Provisioning) to ensure secure and efficient user management.

Use the SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition booster to automate many of the initial setup steps in your subaccount. In addition to the booster, advanced edition requires a set of 'configurator' steps to be completed. These are essential for enabling full functionality of the service (and are not required or present in the standard edition). These steps typically include setting up roles, permissions, and other advanced configurations tailored to the edition’s extended capabilities.

5. Content migration

Since there is no fully automated upgrade or migration flow from the standard to the advanced edition, manual intervention is required. To ease the transition, you can export most of the content from the standard edition and import it into the advanced edition. This process involves the following steps:

  • Exporting the necessary content from the standard edition (as described above)
  • Importing the content to advanced edition

This approach allows for optimization and restructuring where needed. Additionally, leveraging the advanced edition's capabilities, such as interactive workspaces and enhanced page-building tools, can enrich the user experience.

6. Reusing existing configurations

When subscribing to the advanced edition within the same subaccount, certain configurations can be seamlessly reused. BTP destinations, Cloud Connector configurations, and custom applications remain intact and do not require (full) reconfiguration.

If your organization utilizes a custom domain, this can significantly ease the transition from a user perspective. For example, a custom domain that previously pointed to your standard edition site can now be reconfigured to point to the new advanced edition site. This allows users to continue accessing the Work Zone through their existing bookmarks and saved links without disruption. It ensures continuity and minimizes confusion during the switch, while maintaining consistent access points throughout the transition.

7. Best practices

To ensure a smooth transition it's advisable to first perform all migration and configuration activities in a non-production environment, such as a development or testing subaccount. This approach allows for thorough testing, validation of configurations, and identification of potential issues without impacting the live production environment. Once the transition process has been successfully tested and refined in these lower environments, you can confidently replicate the steps in the production subaccount, minimizing the risk of disruptions to end-users.

8. Testing and validation

Conduct thorough User Acceptance Testing (UAT) with a group of end-users to ensure all functionalities work as intended and meet business requirements. Evaluate the system's performance under typical load conditions to identify and address any potential issues.

9. Change management

Develop and deliver training sessions to familiarize users with the new features and interfaces of the advanced edition. Provide documentation, FAQs, and support channels to assist users during the transition period.

10. Go-live

Execute the transition plan, ensuring minimal disruption to daily operations. Continuously monitor the system for any issues and gather user feedback to make necessary adjustments.

Conclusion

This transition can significantly enhance your organization's digital workplace capabilities. By following a structured approach and leveraging the advanced features, you can create a more engaging and efficient user experience. It can empower your teams with the tools they need to collaborate effectively and drive business success.

Hope this blogpost will support your transition journey. We’ll be following up shortly with a detailed how-to guide to help you through the process. 

For more detailed information consider exploring the following:

  • SAP Help Portal: Getting Started with SAP Build Work Zone
  • SAP Build Work Zone on: sap.com / SAP Community
  • SAP Learning Journeys:  Implement and Administer SAP Build Work Zone

entry.title: Say Hi to Joule in SAP SuccessFactors Mobile, Powered by SAP Build - Part 1: Unlocking AI for HR
entry.content:
This two-part blog series explores how SAP SuccessFactors Mobile introduced SAP Joule - SAP's AI copilot - into the hands of mobile users. Part one introduces the opportunity, motivation, and foundational changes that made this integration possible. Part two explores the architectural evolution and how SAP Mobile Services enabled the delivery of this new experience.

SAP SuccessFactors is a market-leading Human Capital Management (HCM) solution, trusted by over 9,900 organizations and more than 260 million users globally. Designed to help organizations attract, develop, and retain their workforce, SAP SuccessFactors offers a comprehensive suite of tools to manage the entire employee lifecycle. 

Complementing the web experience, SAP SuccessFactors Mobile brings HR closer to employees - driving higher engagement, productivity, and informed decision-making. It delivers a native, consumer-grade experience while meeting strict enterprise-grade security standards. With performance and usability at its core, the app is tailored to meet the demands of modern mobile users. 

SAP Joule is an AI copilot grounded in customers' business data and infused with AI agents to proactively assist employees in every cloud application they use - while automating complex processes. 

With the 1H2025 release, SuccessFactors has introduced the Joule experience in its mobile app. This means employees can now interact with the entire enterprise suite using natural language directly from the SuccessFactors mobile app - whether they want to find information, complete a task, or navigate to the right location. 

Joule supports three primary capabilities for HR scenarios: transactional, navigational, and informational.  

  • In transactional scenarios, Joule not only retrieves data - it facilitates actions. For example, when a user says, "I want to request feedback from my manager," Joule collects the necessary context and sends the request directly within the same conversational thread. 
  • In navigational cases, Joule acts as a smart shortcut, directing users to the appropriate screen inside the app. For instance, if you’re unsure where to assign team goals, Joule will generate a direct deep link to the right page. 
  • For informational scenarios, Joule queries its underlying documentation and knowledge base, then presents a concise summary along with related resources. Ask it about your company’s leave policy, and it will display a brief overview plus the top three relevant documents. 

Joule in the SuccessFactors Mobile app isn't limited to HR scenarios. Users can also query data from connected systems like SAP S/4HANA or Ariba - for example, to view open sales orders or pending purchase requests. 

Joule can even be launched via Siri on iOS. Users can say, “Hey Siri, ask Joule in SuccessFactors…,” which opens the app and passes the query to Joule. Joule then processes and responds within the app - creating a seamless, hands-free experience. 

With SuccessFactors' capabilities rapidly expanding on the web, customers will now begin to see the same momentum in the mobile app. Joule's integration empowers users on the go to access not only HR information but also broader SAP business content - bringing the power of one unified copilot across SAP systems into the SuccessFactors mobile experience. 

 

                        

                                              

One of the core goals was to unlock a best-in-class user experience. From the beginning, it was clear that users who had already onboarded to the SuccessFactors mobile app shouldn't need to re-authenticate when accessing Joule. Achieving this required alignment across authentication models. While the mobile app previously used IDP activation protocols, Joule required IAS-based tokens. 

These authentication changes were part of a much larger transformation across the SuccessFactors mobile architecture. In part two, we’ll explore how SAP Mobile Services and its SDKs enabled this journey - and what it means for the future. 

Co-authored by @VinodDevarajan @sandeeptds  @Xeric @SamZhang @SamiLechner @_DineshKumar @ptawade

entry.title: Say Hi to Joule in SAP SuccessFactors Mobile, Powered by SAP Build — Part 2: Architecture, Execution
entry.content:
In the first part of this blog series, we explored how SAP Joule was introduced into the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile app and what it means for users. In this follow-up, we dive deeper into the architecture, execution approach, and the outlook for the mobile experience. 

SAP BTP is a platform engineered to integrate, automate, extend, and build AI-powered business applications and processes across the enterprise. SAP Build is the unified application development and process automation solution on SAP BTP, enabling personalization and efficiency at scale.

SAP Mobile Services is the channel for mobile development in SAP Build. Developers can use the Mobile Development Kit or SAP BTP SDK for iOS or SAP BTP SDK for Android to create robust, native apps. Administrators can manage the full app lifecycle - from configuring push notifications and securing data to enabling offline access and monitoring usage. SAP apps such as SAP Mobile Start, SAP Ariba, and SAP Service and Asset Manager are all built on this foundational technology stack.

 

The SAP BTP SDK for iOS and SAP BTP SDK for Android provide a reusable component enabling SAP apps to deliver a consistent and native Joule experience across mobile platforms. SAP Mobile Start also integrated this component to bring Joule to its users last year.

SAP SuccessFactors Mobile has long leveraged the Fiori frameworks from the BTP SDKs to provide a consumer-grade experience in an enterprise context. These frameworks follow Fiori design guidelines while maintaining a fully native feel on iOS and Android. Given this deep integration with the BTP SDKs, adopting the Joule component was a natural progression.

As outlined in the first blog, SAP SuccessFactors Mobile supports multiple onboarding mechanisms - QR code, deep link, company search, secret code, and MDM-based provisioning. While this flexibility has been key to serving a diverse user base, it also meant that delivering a seamless, SSO-based Joule experience required a significant architectural shift.

To meet this challenge, SuccessFactors leveraged SAP Mobile Services and the SAP BTP SDKs for iOS and Android. This allowed for secure authentication and efficient routing of Joule-related traffic while maintaining compatibility with existing onboarding flows. The change was foundational - requiring careful planning to avoid disruption for millions of active users worldwide. SAP SuccessFactors Mobile app now connects to both the SuccessFactors (for standard HR data/functions via APIs) and to the Joule service for AI queries.

A key decision in the SAP Mobile Services adoption journey was determining how SAP SuccessFactors Mobile Server would communicate with SAP Mobile Services. A lightweight multi-tenancy model was adopted, allowing SAP Mobile Services to operate behind the scenes while preserving the familiar admin experience within SuccessFactors. New APIs were introduced to maintain this continuity while supporting increased scale.

The adoption of SAP Mobile Services had significant implications for SuccessFactors' client logic and the SAP BTP SDKs for iOS and Android. For instance, the SuccessFactors app transitioned to using the SecureStore Module provided by the BTP SDKs, which securely manages and stores sensitive PII user information on mobile devices. By leveraging this module, SuccessFactors eliminated the need to maintain its own database and reduced reliance on third-party libraries. Another example is that SuccessFactors required administrators to configure preferred browsers via MDM and enforce digits-only passcodes. To accommodate these requirements, the SAP BTP SDKs introduced new capabilities in its Foundation and FioriFlows frameworks.

This collaboration offered mutual benefits, as features developed in response to SuccessFactors' needs were implemented in a reusable way, including enhanced MDM support and new authentication capabilities. As a result, these features became part of the Mobile Services platform offering, benefiting not only other SAP apps but also external customers and partners building their own mobile solutions with SAP Build. The effort also enabled SuccessFactors to audit and simplify its own mobile app, streamlining processes in some cases by retiring underused functionality or tightening security – for instance, limiting the ability to delete other profiles to the primary user.

A massive undertaking of this scope required a phased approach from SuccessFactors, even for features already supported. Future releases will enable SuccessFactors to enable Joule on shared devices.

The foundation laid so far has set the stage for long-term collaboration. For example, SuccessFactors' mobile apps will soon feature an updated home page built using BTP Fiori SDK UI components. We're also planning to enable users to capture photos of documents in response to HR flows in Joule for use cases where attaching certificates or receipts is required. Once synergies are fully realized, SuccessFactors will be well-positioned to leverage more SAP Mobile Services capabilities such as offline functionality. Another exciting area of joint exploration is on-device AI models. As mobile devices become increasingly powerful, both teams are keen to investigate how AI workloads can be intelligently distributed between the cloud and the device - unlocking even more responsive and personalized user experiences.

Please note that all future items mentioned here are indicative and may change subject to SAP's strategic interests.

Beyond the features themselves, this collaborative effort continues to drive SAP’s broader strategic vision of being AI-first and Suite-first. With Joule now live in SAP SuccessFactors Mobile, and with a strong outlook ahead, the foundation is set for a smarter, more unified SAP experience - on every device, for every user.

 

Note: This article has been co-authored by @VinodDevarajan @ptawade @Xeric @SamZhang @SamiLechner @_DineshKumar

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build Q2 2025 - Release Highlights
entry.content:
Q2 brought a wave of innovations to SAP Build, including improvements across governance, integration and user experience. We also had several announcements in the spotlight at SAP Sapphire 2025 around new AI capabilities, unified pricing, and of course Joule Studio, which lets you create custom Joule skills and AI agents. In case you missed it, check out our roundup of key updates from SAP Sapphire and the announcement of Joule Studio.

If you’re interested in shaping the future of SAP Build, we’ve introduced the SAP Build Discovery Initiative 2025. We’re looking for participants to meet with product teams, test early prototypes and bring your perspective to influence innovations from day one. Find more info here.

Now let’s dive into the Top 10 release innovations from Q2 2025!

Enhanced governance capabilities

1. Empowering business users to maintain rules through an application

In Process Automation projects, business users are often the content owners of decisions and the logic behind business rules. While the SAP Build Lobby is a powerful environment, it can also be challenging to navigate and maintain the content of separate artifacts. 

Now IT and dev teams can give business users access to decisions via a purpose-built application, which is opened in SAP Build Work Zone. By empowering business users to access the logic this way, it helps mitigate risks of unauthorized changes, while also reducing delays around the change management process.

Manage decisions through a purpose-built application

2. Improved governance with approval flows & multi-node project transport

Strong governance frameworks are essential to ensure that your development activities remain secure and compliant. We have now released two features that support greater control and quality assurance:

  • Governance approval flows: This enables administrators to control who is authorized to create or transport projects across tenants or sub-accounts. The approval process is fully customizable, allowing organizations to implement quality gates aligned with their specific governance and compliance requirements.
  • Multi-node project transport: Approvers can now move SAP Build projects (apps, processes, automations) through a controlled Dev -> Test -> Production landscape, with approval steps and quality checks at each stage. This ensures dev teams are moving projects securely and efficiently across tenants.

Governance approval flows

Seamless interoperability and AI-powered integration

3. Importing Business Process Model & Notation (BPMN) to generate process artifacts automatically 

Great news for our automation enthusiasts – it’s now possible to import BPMN processes from other tools into SAP Build! BPMN is the industry standard for defining workflows, decisions, and interactions across systems and teams. 

Now process designers can migrate existing BPMN processes from SAP Signavio, SAP NetWeaver, and IBM to generate process artifacts automatically. This long-awaited transportability helps to speed up the logic creation significantly, as well as acting as the bridge between SAP Build and other process modeling tools. 

To get started with BPMN process imports, see the Help Portal.Import BPMN processes into SAP Build

4. Improved SAP SuccessFactors integration with event discovery

From the SAP Build Lobby, developers can manage and connect SAP and non-SAP systems through the Unified Customer Landscape (UCL). Now in the UCL, developers can discover business events from SAP Cloud Application Event Hub, as well as SAP SuccessFactors solutions. This improved integration supports more relevant scenarios for HR processes, such as employee onboarding and compensation management. 

5. AI grounding in company-specific content for tailored responses

This feature makes it possible to base Joule copilot and other embedded AI capabilities on up-to-date documents and user-created content in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition through the RAG (retrieval augmented generation) method. Rather than giving generic responses, it provides end users with more accurate and domain-specific content.

AI grounding within Joule copilot

6. Joule-based assistant to easily migrate SAP HANA applications to CAP

For simplified innovation across services, the SAP HANA Application Migration Assistant is a new extension available in SAP Build Code. This automates the conversion of XS classic applications into CAP-based services to accelerate modernization and help businesses adopt cloud-native architectures faster.

For more info, see Product Updates for SAP Build Code – June 2025 Edition

Optimized user experience and accessibility across devices

7. SAP Mobile Start as the unified mobile access point for users

SAP Mobile Start addresses the challenge of fragmented access to enterprise applications and information for mobile users. The connection of SAP Mobile Start with SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition ensures that users have consistent and secure access to business applications, tasks, and notifications, regardless of their location or device.

 8. Preview SAPUI5 updates for better UX management

To address the need for more controlled testing, we’ve introduced the ability to preview upcoming SAPUI5 versions in non-productive environments within SAP Build Work Zone. Admins can activate the preview in a staging or dev tenant, validate compatibility and confirm everything works as expected before go-live – ensuring agile development, reduced downtime, and better user experience management.

For more info, read the blog post.

SAPUI5 version preview in SAP Build Work Zone

9. Local Entry Point (LEP) to simplify access to standalone sites

The new Local Entry Point (LEP) concept of SAP Build Work Zone simplifies how SaaS application providers integrate business solutions into SAP BTP. Providers can generate a standalone entry-point site in the consumer’s subaccount, eliminating the extra efforts and complexity required to set up their own instances.

To get started, see the Help Portal.

10. Updates to Mobile Development Kit for enhanced UI, APIs and more

The latest release to SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) has brought several new updates for customers, including the newest version of Mobile Services Client in the Google Play Store and Apple App Store. Among the newest enhancements to UI is support for donut charts, providing a new way to visualize data and emphasize different aspects of datasets.

See all the latest updates: What’s New in Mobile Development Kit client 25.6

Ready to see these in action?

To learn more and see live demos of the latest release highlights, join us for the next What’s New in SAP Build Q2 2025 Webinar! 

👉Register for your preferred time slot:

  • July 17th, 9:00AM – 10:00AM CEST: https://sap.to/60414diDL
  • July 17th, 5:00PM – 6:00PM CEST: https://sap.to/60434diDF
